rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes when bounds are concrete and visible.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features.
Generate boolean range helpers with explicit type alignment to avoid assumed/actual type mismatches.
Omit range assumptions for Boolean or opaque data ports unless numeric scalar representation exists.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex agree blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Component type contracts govern port behaviors; implementation contracts handle subcomponent bindings.
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly typed in scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq` for helper declarations with explicit boolean type annotations.
Place `assign` statements only in implementation scopes with valid output features.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE expressions are declared or visible in the component scope.
Wrap AGREE annex content in `annex agree {** ... **};` block syntax.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes.
Do not assume Boolean ports can be compared numerically without scalar representation.
Do not reference AADL timing properties directly in AGREE expressions.
Do not generate range assumptions without visible feature or constant bounds.
